summary: Successfully tested Next.js chat page refactoring - all components extracted properly, line count under 120, React.memo() implemented, and UI functionality verified

design_issues.issue: Clerk configuration modal appears on page load (development keys warning)
design_issues.note: This is expected in development mode and doesn't affect functionality

passed_tests: ✅ types/chat.ts exists and exports VoiceState, ConversationEntry, PersonalityKey, PERSONALITY_OPTIONS
✅ VoiceButton.tsx exists, uses React.memo(), has correct props interface
✅ StatusDisplay.tsx exists, uses React.memo(), displays status text per state
✅ SettingsPanel.tsx exists, uses React.memo(), has personality selector and voice toggle
✅ ConversationLog.tsx exists, uses React.memo() with custom comparator, virtualizes at 20+ messages
✅ ParticleVisualizer.tsx exists, uses React.memo(), has quality scaling and visibility API listener
✅ Chat page renders at localhost:3000/chat with all required components
✅ All data-testid attributes present: particle-visualizer, voice-tap-area, settings-panel, voice-button, voice-status-text, idle-status-hint
✅ Settings panel opens when settings-toggle-btn is clicked
✅ All personality options visible and functional (bestfriend, professional, playful, mentor)
✅ No inline type definitions remain in app/chat/page.tsx
✅ Particle visualizer rendering correctly with Three.js
✅ Voice button shows correct idle state indicator
✅ Status display shows correct text ('Hey' and 'Tap anywhere to speak')

success_percentage.backend: N/A - No backend testing required for this refactoring task
